1.1: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy the brother, To Philemon, our beloved and fellow worker,
1.2: and to Apphia the sister, and to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church at your house:
1.3: Grace to you? and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
1.4: I thank my God always, making remembrance of you in my prayers,
1.5: hearing of your love and the faith that you have toward the Lord Jesus and for all the saints,
1.6: so that the fellowship of your faith might become effective in the full knowledge of every good thing that is in us in Christ.
1.7: For I have much joy and encouragement on the basis of your love, because the inner parts of the saints have been refreshed by you, brother.
1.8: Therefore, having much boldness in Christ to order you to do what is fitting,
1.9: because of love, rather, I exhort you , being such a one as Paul, now an elderly man and also a prisoner of Christ Jesus.
1.10: I exhort you concerning my child whom I begot in chains, Onesimus,
1.11: the one once useless to you, but now useful both to you and to me,
1.12: whom I sent back to you himself—this one who is my inner parts—
1.13: whom I was wishing to keep with myself, so that on behalf of you he might minister to me in the chains of the gospel.
1.14: But apart from your consent I wanted to do nothing, so that your good may be not as according to necessity, but according to willingness.
1.15: For perhaps because of this he was separated for an hour, so that you might have him back eternally,
1.16: no longer as a slave, but above a slave, a beloved brother—especially to me, but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
1.17: Therefore, if you hold me a partner, receive him as me.
1.18: But if in any way he wronged you or he owes you , impute this to me.
1.19: I, Paul, wrote with my own hand. I will repay it , that I may not say to you that you also owe to me even yourself.
1.20: Yes, brother, may I have profit from you in the Lord. Refresh my inner parts in Christ.
1.21: Being persuaded of your obedience, I wrote to you, knowing that you will do even above what I say.
1.22: Now at the same time, also prepare a lodging for me; for I hope that through your? prayers I will be graciously given to you?.
1.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
1.24: as do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
1.25: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your? spirit.
